title: French PM resolves to step up economic aid as Middle East conflict drags on
subhead: Sébastien Lecornu plans to unveil new measures to support economic activity. He said the sustained rise in oil prices means France must 'change scale,' even if it makes the country's already tough budget situation more complicated.

subhead: The French president, who began his first term aiming to ease historical tensions, now faces the most severe crisis in bilateral relations since Algeria's independence in 1962.

title: Sweden's conservatives are now normalizing the far-right
subhead: With legislative elections scheduled for September 13 approaching, the Conservatives, Liberals and Christian Democrats have signaled their willingness to govern with the Sweden Democrats, the far-right party that enabled them to return to power in 2022.

title: German Chancellor Merz hits a new low one year after taking office
subhead: At the helm of a coalition plagued by disagreements and criticized for his verbal gaffes, the German head of government is the most unpopular chancellor since the founding of the Federal Republic.

title: Scottish elections: Reform UK capitalizes on anti-migrant protests in Falkirk
subhead: As regional elections take place on Thursday, May 7, in Scotland and Wales, far-right activists have managed to rally broad support around slogans hostile to asylum seekers in a region previously known for being welcoming.

title: Europeans rethink collective defense amid Trump's threats against NATO
subhead: Would the US intervene in the event of an attack on Europe? European leaders, who met Monday in Yerevan alongside Canada, are growing increasingly doubtful after Trump announced the withdrawal of 5,000 American troops from Germany.
